mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate above a 0.5 percentage point threshold over three months signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation creates stagflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both exceed their 6-month moving averages, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening corporate output and supply chain normalization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery periods, with a rolling Sharpe ratio below 0.5 over 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic activity and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across all market cycles, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1.5 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of cases when HOUST breaches the 1500 threshold, invalidating the recessionary signal.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and rising unemployment indicates a policy-induced economic slowdown, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and UNRATE shows no statistical significance in predicting negative returns, with p-values exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth confirms expanding corporate revenue streams and profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during expansion periods, with a hit rate below 50% over a 5-year backtest.
